APIErrorCode.description:
Codes emitted by the core API.

Adding or changing a code changes the committed OpenAPI snapshot and therefore requires an
explicit contract review.
APIErrorCode.title: APIErrorCode

APIErrorResponse.description: Additive error response shared by all ``/api`` routes.

detail.description: Human-readable detail; clients must not parse this value.
detail.title: Detail

retryable.description: Whether the server knows that retrying the same request is safe under this operation's semantics.
retryable.title: Retryable

APIErrorResponse.title: APIErrorResponse

BackendInfo.description:
An execution backend and the workers it currently offers (powers the real Compute view — the
honest replacement for the hardcoded `warm`).
BackendInfo.title: BackendInfo

CapabilityView.description:
A plugin capability that contributes a VIEWER TAB, declaratively. `viewer.kind` names a generic
renderer the SPA ships (e.g. 'grid' = media/image grid, 'json' = pretty-printed cell) — so a plugin
adds a viewer tab (for columns it tags via its detector) with NO frontend code, the same way a
NodeSpec renders a node card. See kernel/hub/plugins/capabilities.py + web/src/nodes/capabilities.
CapabilityView.title: CapabilityView

CatalogBrowse.description:
One level of the browse tree at a prefix: the immediate child folders (with subtree counts) and
the tables filed directly at this prefix — a bounded sample (`total_tables`/`truncated` signal
when there are more; the full listing is the paginated list query with folder=prefix). Lets the
UI lazily expand a folder tree of any size.
CatalogBrowse.title: CatalogBrowse

CatalogEdit.description: One complete staged built-in catalog edit, guarded by its read revision.
CatalogEdit.title: CatalogEdit

CatalogFolder.description:
A first-class browse folder. Additive to the per-dataset `folder` path string: it lets an EMPTY
folder exist and be renamed/deleted, so a folder can be created up front and filled later.
CatalogFolder.title: CatalogFolder

CatalogMetadata.description:
The owner-editable organization fields of a dataset (everything but the probed schema/rows).
A PUT of this is how a table gets filed into a folder, tagged, owned, or described.
CatalogMetadata.title: CatalogMetadata

CatalogPage.description:
One window of a filtered catalog: the page's items plus the totals a UI needs to paginate
(total match count, whether more follow) — so the client shows '1–50 of 4,213' and loads the
next page on demand instead of holding every table in memory.
CatalogPage.title: CatalogPage

distinct.description: Measured distinct count, or null when the column type cannot be compared.
distinct.title: Distinct

distinctIsApproximate.description: Whether distinct was estimated (for example, whole-dataset HLL) rather than exact.
distinctIsApproximate.title: Distinctisapproximate

ColumnSchema.description:
One current schema-field model.

``type`` is the logical type used by execution and the UI.  Adapters may also
report their source physical type, but must leave facts they cannot prove as
``None`` rather than guessing.

fieldId.description: Stable source-supplied field identity, when available.
fieldId.title: Fieldid

hasDefault.description: Whether a non-null field has a source default; null means unknown.
hasDefault.title: Hasdefault

nullable.description: Whether the field accepts null values; null means unknown.
nullable.title: Nullable

provenance.description: Evidence source for this field metadata.
provenance.title: Provenance

ColumnSchema.title: ColumnSchema

DatasetRevisionCapabilities.description: Provider-advertised revision selectors and their portable ordering contract.
DatasetRevisionCapabilities.title: DatasetRevisionCapabilities

DatasetRevisionDetail.description: Inspectable facts and a bounded preview for one retained exact revision.
DatasetRevisionDetail.title: DatasetRevisionDetail

DatasetRevisionLastKnown.description: Bounded non-sensitive display evidence saved with an exact Source selection.
DatasetRevisionLastKnown.title: DatasetRevisionLastKnown

DatasetRevisionPreview.description: A fixed, exact-revision preview window; it is never a read of current head.
DatasetRevisionPreview.title: DatasetRevisionPreview

DatasetRevisionResolution.description: Evidence that a latest/as-of request resolved to an immutable native revision.
DatasetRevisionResolution.title: DatasetRevisionResolution

DatasetRevisionSummary.description: Bounded provider facts for one exact revision; absent facts are not inferred.
DatasetRevisionSummary.title: DatasetRevisionSummary

DurableBoundedFanoutView.description: Sanitized parent-only bounded fan-out projection for Workspace Jobs.
DurableBoundedFanoutView.title: DurableBoundedFanoutView

DurableCheckpointView.description: Sanitized, path-free checkpoint projection for Workspace Jobs.
DurableCheckpointView.title: DurableCheckpointView

DurableTaskInboxItemView.description: One personal Inbox outcome for a terminal certified durable TaskAttempt.
DurableTaskInboxItemView.title: DurableTaskInboxItemView

ExactDatasetRef.description: Opaque, path-independent identity for one exact retained dataset revision.
ExactDatasetRef.title: ExactDatasetRef

Facets.description:
Distinct values + counts for each facetable dimension, computed over the ACTIVE filter set
(drill-down semantics) — what powers the facet rail's clickable, counted filters.
`semantic_available` rides along so a UI knows whether search-by-meaning exists (an embedder
plugin is installed) without a separate capability round-trip.
Facets.title: Facets

FolderNode.description: A folder in the browse tree: its leaf name, full path, and how many tables live in its subtree.
FolderNode.title: FolderNode

InputDriftRequest.description: Compare a retained preview binding with current provider heads without changing either.
InputDriftRequest.title: InputDriftRequest

JoinAnalysis.description:
Everything the join node's inspector needs: ranked key suggestions for its two inputs, and a
fan-out warning when the join isn't 1:1 (the result lands at the finer grain — a later
parent-grain metric would double-count unless you aggregate).
JoinAnalysis.title: JoinAnalysis

JoinSuggestion.description:
A proposed way to join two datasets: matching key column(s) on each side + the measured
join cardinality. Surfaced in the join node's inspector (catalog-driven join hints).
JoinSuggestion.title: JoinSuggestion

KeyInfo.description:
A candidate/known key of a dataset — the column(s) that identify a row (a primary key).
Composite = more than one column. `confidence`: 'declared' (owner-asserted) | 'verified'
(measured unique on the data) | 'inferred' (name heuristic, unmeasured).
KeyInfo.title: KeyInfo

LineagePublication.description:
Catalog-local provenance supplied when one output becomes visible.

This is deliberately narrower than an execution request: it contains only the immutable identity
needed to create one fact per source in the catalog transaction. ``attempt_id`` remains null when a
backend has no execution attempt distinct from the logical run.
LineagePublication.title: LineagePublication

PluginStatus.description: Bounded operator-facing status; implementation exceptions and tracebacks are intentionally absent.
PluginStatus.title: PluginStatus

ProfileEstimate.description: Whole-profile preflight plus the server-minted identity required by submission.
ProfileEstimate.title: ProfileEstimate

ProfileEstimateRequest.description: Estimate a whole-dataset profile before the user chooses whether to submit it.
ProfileEstimateRequest.title: ProfileEstimateRequest

ProfileIdentity.description: Current server identity for recovery without re-running the size estimate.
ProfileIdentity.title: ProfileIdentity

ProfileIdentityRequest.description: Compute the current server identity for one node without starting work.
ProfileIdentityRequest.title: ProfileIdentityRequest

ProfileJobRequest.description:
Submit a whole-dataset profile through the durable job lifecycle.

``plan_digest`` is minted by the server preflight and checked again at submission. The fixed wire
value is persisted so a late result cannot be presented for a newer graph or source revision without
duplicating the submitted graph in durable status rows.
ProfileJobRequest.title: ProfileJobRequest

ProfileResult.description: Column statistics with an explicit, non-inferred measurement scope.

completeness.description: complete=whole dataset, sample=bounded rows, unknown=no statistics are available.
completeness.title: Completeness

rowCount.description: Rows actually profiled; a full dataset total only when completeness=complete.
rowCount.title: Rowcount

sampled.description: Whether statistics were computed from a bounded sample instead of all rows.
sampled.title: Sampled

ProfileResult.title: ProfileResult

Relationship.description:
A declared relationship between two datasets — the user-asserted join edge shown in the ER
view. `confidence='declared'` (owner-asserted, trusted like a real FK). Composite via the column
lists. This is the escape hatch for opaque transforms: declare what the code produced.
Relationship.title: Relationship

ResourceSpec.description:
A compute-resource shape, used BOTH ways: a worker advertises its `capacity`, a step declares
its `requires`. A worker satisfies a step when its capacity ⊇ the requirement (hub.placement,
Phase C). All fields optional — an empty spec means "no particular requirement / unspecified".
ResourceSpec.title: ResourceSpec

RunBackendRef.description:
Durable handle for a run owned by an external execution control plane.

The handle is intentionally provider-neutral and contains no credentials. A backend can reconstruct
its supervisor from this record plus its own operator configuration after the hub/kernel restarts.
RunBackendRef.title: RunBackendRef

RunHistoryRecord.description: Bounded, explicit response model for one durable run-history row.
RunHistoryRecord.title: RunHistoryRecord

RunOutput.description:
One declared output port and its durable publication state.

Port metadata is a snapshot, not a live lookup: history remains intelligible after a node spec or
Section declaration changes.  A URI becomes public only after publication commits; pending and
terminal non-committed outcomes therefore cannot carry storage or catalog identities.
RunOutput.title: RunOutput

RunOutputSampleRequest.description: A run-owned artifact page request; the URI is resolved server-side from durable metadata.
RunOutputSampleRequest.title: RunOutputSampleRequest

SampleProvenance.description:
Evidence for the population a visible preview/sample page represents.

``scanned_rows`` and ``total_rows`` are intentionally nullable: a bounded adapter may prove its
work limit without knowing how many rows it actually inspected or how large the population is.
``identity`` changes when any known sampling input changes, so a client can distinguish samples
that happen to contain similar rows.
SampleProvenance.title: SampleProvenance

SampleResult.description:
One page of rows plus an explicit statement of what that page represents.

``row_count`` is an exact total when present, never the number of rows in this response. A limit
applies either to EACH upstream source scan or to the browsable RESULT window; it is deliberately
separate from the caller-selected page size. This prevents a bounded multi-source preview from
being described as the first N output rows.

completeness.description: complete=all rows; page=one page of a known/continuable result; sample=derived from bounded source scans; capped=result window ended at a server limit; unknown=no proof.
completeness.title: Completeness

hasMore.description: Whether another page exists within the current interactive scope: true/false is proven by lookahead, exact metadata, or a result-window boundary; null is unknown. It never asserts whether more rows exist in the full dataset beyond that scope.
hasMore.title: Hasmore

limitReason.description: Why rowLimit applies. Present only together with rowLimit and limitScope.
limitReason.title: Limitreason

limitScope.description: each-source means every upstream source scan is independently bounded; result-window means result pagination cannot continue beyond rowLimit.
limitScope.title: Limitscope

rowCount.description: Exact total rows in the dataset/result when known; not this page's row count.
rowCount.title: Rowcount

rowLimit.description: Server row limit whose meaning is defined by limitScope; never the page size.
rowLimit.title: Rowlimit

truncated.description: Whether this response is not a proven complete dataset/result. Every successful non-complete response is truncated; unavailable responses are not.
truncated.title: Truncated

SampleResult.title: SampleResult

detail.description: Human-readable detail; clients must not parse this value.
detail.title: Detail

retryable.description: Whether the server knows that retrying the same request is safe under this operation's semantics.
retryable.title: Retryable

TransformLibraryEntry.description: Public metadata for one exact Transform version; executable code is never exposed.
TransformLibraryEntry.title: TransformLibraryEntry

WorkerInfo.description:
One execution slot in a backend — a pod, a process, or the local host (the backend decides
which). `capacity` is what it advertises; Phase C's scheduler matches a step's requires against it.
WorkerInfo.title: WorkerInfo

WorkspaceResource.description: One Workspace child addressed by an opaque, path-independent reference.
WorkspaceResource.title: WorkspaceResource

WorkspaceRunRecord.description: One visible run in the workspace-wide, read-only Jobs projection.
WorkspaceRunRecord.title: WorkspaceRunRecord

WorkspaceSourceStatus.description: Completeness of one independently bounded source in a mixed Workspace page.
WorkspaceSourceStatus.title: WorkspaceSourceStatus

WriteDestination.description: Stable logical identity of one managed write destination.
WriteDestination.title: WriteDestination

WriteIntent.description: Frozen pre-1.0 contract for one managed local create, replace, or Lance append.
WriteIntent.title: WriteIntent

WritePartitionExpectation.description: One bounded partition field requested by a write intent.
WritePartitionExpectation.title: WritePartitionExpectation

WriteProvenance.description: Frozen producer evidence and the ordered source identities used by one write.
WriteProvenance.title: WriteProvenance

WritePublicationIdentity.description: Durable provider-side identity of one managed local publication.
WritePublicationIdentity.title: WritePublicationIdentity

WriteReceipt.description: Durable evidence for one exact revision-producing write.
WriteReceipt.title: WriteReceipt

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

schema.title: Response Put Canvas Api Canvas  Canvas Id  Put

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

get.description:
In-flight runs for a canvas, so a reopened canvas re-subscribes to a run that survived a hub
restart on its kernel (rather than the run silently vanishing from the UI). Rebuilt into RunStatus
so it serializes with the same camelCase wire shape as GET /run/{id}.

schema.title: Response Canvas Active Runs Api Canvas  Canvas Id  Active Runs Get

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

get.description:
The per-canvas execution kernel's live state (Jupyter-style), or {exists:false} if none is
running. Token/endpoint are internal — only lease state + the kernel's own /status are surfaced.
Read-only: this NEVER spawns a kernel, and the /status proxy fast-fails so a dead kernel can't
stall the request.

schema.title: Response Canvas Kernel Api Canvas  Canvas Id  Kernel Get

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

post.description:
Restart the canvas's kernel (Jupyter's 'Restart kernel'): shut the current one down; the next
run/preview spawns a fresh one. A wedged transform or a stale warm state is cleared this way.

schema.title: Response Canvas Kernel Restart Api Canvas  Canvas Id  Kernel Restart Post

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

get.description:
Latest durable profile attempt for each node/plan identity, including terminal attempts.

Unlike ``active-runs``, this bounded recovery surface lets a profile that finished while the canvas
was closed reappear on reopen. The client still verifies the fixed plan digest against its current
graph identity before presenting the result.

schema.title: Response Canvas Profile Jobs Api Canvas  Canvas Id  Profile Jobs Get

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

schema.title: Response Restore Canvas Api Canvas  Canvas Id  Restore Post

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

get.description: Run history for a canvas (persisted, survives restarts).

schema.title: Response Canvas Runs Api Canvas  Canvas Id  Runs Get

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

get.description: Inspect one immutable History/Jobs manifest under current Canvas visibility.

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

schema.title: Response Add Share Api Canvas  Canvas Id  Share Post

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

schema.title: Response Remove Share Api Canvas  Canvas Id  Share  User Id  Delete

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

schema.title: Response Get Shares Api Canvas  Canvas Id  Shares Get

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

get.description: Return safe exact metadata only for library refs already present in a readable Canvas.

schema.title: Response List Canvas Transform References Api Canvas  Canvas Id  Transform References Get

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

schema.title: Response Get Canvas Versions Api Canvas  Canvas Id  Versions Get

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

get.description:
Distinct folder / tag / owner values + counts over the active filter set — powers the facet rail
(each value is a one-click, counted filter). Computed with the SAME filters as the list, so the
counts always describe what a click would show. `semanticAvailable` rides along so the UI knows
whether a search-by-meaning mode exists (an embedder plugin is installed).

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

get.description:
Every folder entity — including EMPTY ones. Powers the folder-name autocomplete (unioned with the
entry-derived folder facets on the client). Empty when the provider owns no local folder store.

schema.title: Response List Folders Api Catalog Folders Get

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

post.description: Create an EMPTY folder (fill it later). 409 if it already exists, 400 if the path is invalid.

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

post.description: Delete a folder, moving its datasets + subfolders up to the parent (structure preserved). 400 if unknown.

schema.title: Response Delete Folder Api Catalog Folders Delete Post

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

put.description:
Rename a folder, cascading to every dataset and subfolder under it. Works whether the folder is a
created entity or exists only because a dataset was registered into it. 400 on unknown/collision.

schema.title: Response Rename Folder Api Catalog Folders Rename Put

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

post.description:
Ranked ways to join two catalog datasets, with cardinality MEASURED on the data (see
hub.relationships) — the catalog-driven 'how do these join?' hint.

schema.title: Response Join Suggestions Api Catalog Join Suggestions Post

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

get.description:
The lineage component around `uri`, expanded breadth-first from the store and CAPPED by `depth`
+ `max_nodes` so a large graph can't blow up the payload (`truncated` flags that the cap hit).

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

get.description: Export immutable provenance facts with a deletion-safe, monotonic keyset cursor.

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.
API error with a stable code and retry classification.

schema.title: Response List Relationships Api Catalog Relationships Get

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

schema.title: Response Add Relationship Api Catalog Relationships Post

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

post.description: POST (not DELETE) — the relationship identity is a body, and DELETE-with-body is unreliable.

schema.title: Response Delete Relationship Api Catalog Relationships Delete Post

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

get.description: Return bounded facts and preview for one exact revision; never fall back to current head.

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

get.description:
Search the catalog. `mode`: 'lexical' (name/folder/tag/column substring), 'semantic' (embedding
similarity — active only when a plugin registered an embedder), or 'hybrid' (both, rank-fused).
Structured filters use the same CatalogQuery contract in every mode. Falls back to lexical when no
embedder is installed, so search always works offline.

schema.title: Response Catalog Search Api Catalog Search Get

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

get.description: A filtered, sorted, paginated catalog page with its window and total in the response body.

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

post.description:
Best-effort, bounded removal with one metadata CAS precondition per dataset.

Results are deliberately per-item: one stale or concurrently busy dataset never hides which
other removals committed. Providers without revision-aware unregister remain read-only here.

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

delete.description: Remove one exact dataset only while its editable metadata still matches the caller's read.

schema.title: Response Unregister Table Api Catalog Tables  Table Id  Delete

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.
API error with a stable code and retry classification.

put.description:
Atomically save a staged built-in table edit, guarded by its opaque CAS revision.

This route intentionally does not extend the external CatalogProvider SPI. A provider that does
not ship the same built-in implementation is truthful rather than accepting two separate writes.

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

put.description:
Set (or clear, with []) a dataset's owner-declared primary key — leads its keys and wins in
grain. The escape hatch when the name heuristic misses or an opaque transform produced the data.

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

put.description:
Curate a dataset's organization — file it into a `folder`, set `tags`, an `owner`, a
`description`. Only the fields PRESENT in the body change (absent → untouched); an explicit
null (or "") on owner/description CLEARS it. The probed schema/rows are untouched.

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

get.description: A bounded newest-first page of provider-native history for one current registration.

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

get.description: Expose only provider-declared revision selectors and portable ordering semantics.

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

get.description: Resolve latest or an as-of instant to immutable provider evidence without opening head later.

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

get.description:
One level of the folder/browse tree at `prefix`: immediate child folders (with subtree counts) +
the tables filed directly here. Lets a UI lazily expand a folder tree of any size.

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

post.description:
Upload a dataset file's bytes and register it. The raw request body IS the file; its name comes in
the X-Upload-Filename header. Bytes STREAM to a temp file, capped at DP_MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES as they arrive
(so an oversized upload is aborted without being buffered anywhere — no reliance on Content-Length),
then land in shared storage (a local dir, or object storage when DP_STORAGE_URL is set — visible to
every instance) and register into the cross-instance catalog, returned as a CatalogTable.

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

post.description:
The execution plan for a target: the regions it splits into, each with backend + boundary tier +
estimated size — the UI 'run plan' preview that makes cost-based placement + tiering visible. A plain
graph is one 'default' region (runs locally); placement (a cluster backend / engine label / checkpoint)
splits it. Never 500s.

schema.title: Response Graph Plan Api Graph Plan Post

get.description: Stream the complete, already-materialized native artifact without buffering or conversion.

200.description: Complete native result artifact. The runtime preserves its Parquet, Arrow, CSV, TSV, or JSON media type and streams without conversion.

Cache-Control.description: private, no-store; full-result bytes are never cached by shared intermediaries.

Content-Disposition.description: A sanitized filename ending in -full-result plus the native extension.

Content-Length.description: Exact native artifact byte size when available.

X-Content-Type-Options.description: Always nosniff for downloaded native data.

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.

head.description: Authorize and open the exact native member so preflight reports every GET error before download.

200.description: Full-result export preflight. Performs the same authorization, output resolution, manifest validation, and native member open as GET; returns its media, filename, and size headers without a response body.

Cache-Control.description: private, no-store; full-result bytes are never cached by shared intermediaries.

Content-Disposition.description: A sanitized filename ending in -full-result plus the native extension.

Content-Length.description: Exact native artifact byte size when available.

X-Content-Type-Options.description: Always nosniff for downloaded native data.

default.description: API error with a stable code and retry classification.
